round_summary: All three agents demonstrate strong cooperative signaling with mutual acknowledgment, reciprocal praise, and commitment to continued restraint as the commons recovers to 70%, with Agent 0 attempting to initiate a formal shared target norm.

Agents 0 and 1 are incrementally adding while jointly sanctioning Agent 2's disproportionately large herd of 19, invoking fairness and long-term sustainability norms, yet Agent 2 deflects accountability by emphasizing pasture health and only vaguely signaling openness to change.

Agents 0 and 2 demonstrate active convergence toward a shared norm of balanced herds (~13-15), while Agent 1 free-rides on others' reductions by maintaining its higher level despite verbal affirmations of cooperation.

All three agents display strong mutual reinforcement of an established cooperative equilibrium, citing empirical resource health as validation of their shared restraint norm, with no defection signals present.

All three agents send near-identical messages affirming a stable, mutually beneficial 14-cow equilibrium backed by near-perfect pasture health, demonstrating strong collective norm reinforcement and trust maintenance.
